title: 10 best herbal essential oils for lymph node swelling
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for lymph node swelling are natural remedies that help alleviate swelling and inflammation of the lymph nodes. They work by stimulating the lymphatic system, reducing pain and promoting healing. The best herbal essential oils for this condition include Echinacea purpurea, Echinacea angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Silybum marianum, Ginkgo biloba, Calendula officinalis, Thuja occidentalis, Zingiber officinale, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, lymph node swelling can lead to chronic fatigue, immune system suppression, and even organ damage, significantly impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with lymph node swelling because of its unique ability to stimulate lymphatic circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote detoxification. The oil's citral content has been shown to increase the production of lymphatic enzymes, which helps to break down and eliminate toxins from the body. Additionally, geranium essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with lymph node enlargement, making it a natural and effective remedy for promoting healthy lymphatic function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Geranium essential oil's antioxidant property helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to lymph node swelling by preventing damage to surrounding tissues.

Anti-inflammatory property: Geranium essential oil's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory hormones that can exacerbate lymph node swelling.

Stimulatory property: Geranium essential oil's stimulatory property helps to stimulate the lymphatic system, improving its function and promoting the removal of toxins and waste products that can accumulate in swollen lymph nodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Geranium roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce lymph node swelling by promoting healthy immune function.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of geranium plants contain natural diuretics that help alleviate fluid retention and swelling in the lymph nodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves in the early morning for optimal oil content and quality.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the washed leaves at 100°c for 4 hours using a clevenger apparatus to extract essential oil.
Monitor the distillation process carefully and collect the extracted oil as it is separated from water using a hydrometer.
Filter the collected oil through a cotton cloth or paper filter to remove any impurities or sediment.

Leaves: As the primary photosynthetic organs, leaves contain high concentrations of volatile oils, which can be extracted to create essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating lymph node swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or early morning when oil content is highest.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels below 10%.
Use a steam distillation apparatus with 0.5 kg of dried melissa leaves and 1 l of distilled water at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, keeping away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a clean environment ensuring proper handling.
Chop the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves into smaller pieces for efficient steam distillation.
Combine 100g of chopped eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500ml of distilled water in a stainless steel pot.
Steam distill the mixture for 3 hours at atmospheric pressure and moderate heat to extract essential oil.
Separate the extracted essential oil from the distillate using a separation funnel and store in a dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested material for at least 14 days in a warm well-ventilated area.
Steam distill the dried herbs using 100 milliliters of water for every 500 milligrams of plant material.
Collect the essential oil after 30 minutes of distillation and filter it through a coffee filter paper.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom at dawn or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in small batches using a dehydrator on low heat for 3-5 hours or air-drying for several days.
Combine dried flowers with carrier oil in a 1:4 ratio and store the mixture in an airtight container for 14-21 days to allow infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.
Use the resulting essential oil undiluted or mixed with a carrier oil in a 1:10 ratio, applying topically 2-3 times daily to affected areas or inhaling through steam inhalation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus.
Steam distill the harvested leaves with water at a ratio of 1:4 for 3 hours.
Separate the resulting essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator or cheesecloth.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final cymbopogon citratus essential oil in dark glass bottles away from heat sources.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at dawn and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation for 24 hours or oven drying at 35°c for 4 hours.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a clevenger apparatus with 1:5 leaf-to-water ratio for 4 hours.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the water and filter it through paper towels to remove impurities.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried origanum majorana leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.

Steam distill the dried origanum majorana leaves using 10 pounds of leaves with 1 gallon of water at 100°f for 3 hours.
Filter and decant the distilled oil into glass bottles, discarding any sediment or impurities after 24 hours.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves at dawn in summer months weighing about 250 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves using a dehydrator on low heat for exactly 12 hours or air dry for two weeks.
Steam distill dried salvia officinalis leaves with water at a ratio of one part herb to four parts water for three hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distilled water through sedimentation and decantation methods.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles sealed tightly and kept at room temperature for up to six months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers at dawn or dusk for optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Mix one pound of dried calendula flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at room temperature.
Steam distill the mixture in a steam distillation apparatus or a ceramic still at 160°f for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oils.
Strain the resulting essential oil from the carrier oil and filter through a coffee filter or cheesecloth before bottling for use.
